input.counterSource: W3C CSS WG blog (w3.org/blog/CSS/2025/03/28/css-grid-1-2-3-updated/) and CSS-Tricks (css-tricks.com/masonry-layout-is-now-grid-lanes/) document the 2025 resolution in favor of grid-lanes, showing the debate has since concluded — but this does not contradict the claim's late-2024 framing.
input.evidence: The claim is directly and accurately supported by the primary source. The WebKit blog post at webkit.org/blog/16026/css-masonry-syntax/ (published 2024) explicitly describes two competing proposals: a Grid Integrated option (display: grid; grid-template-rows: masonry) and a Grid Independent option (display: masonry; masonry-template-tracks). Multiple independent corroborating sources confirm this — the W3C CSS WG published the First Public Working Draft of CSS Grid Level 3 in September 2024 containing both options, Chrome DevRel published parallel coverage at developer.chrome.com/blog/masonry-syntax, and secondary analysis at CSS-Tricks, Smashing Magazine, and OddBird all confirm the two-proposal framing as of late 2024. The claim is time-scoped to "late 2024," which is accurate — the debate was genuinely live and unresolved at that time. By late 2025 the CSSWG resolved in favor of a grid-integrated approach renamed "CSS Grid Lanes" (display: grid-lanes), but that post-dates the claim's stated timeframe and does not retroactively make the 2024 characterization false. Source quality is high: webkit.org is a primary implementer and CSS WG participant, not a press release or forum speculation.
